в•‘      Almaaz Belt     Sulafat Haze     Yed Posterior     в•‘
в•‘   Muphrid ambush   Saltwind Ice Shelf   Edasich Halo    в•‘
в•ҡв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•қ

At Keelbreak I, the rocks advertise themselves as innocence:
Uncut Gems вҖ” Keelbreak,
as if the knife were not already implied in the name of the system.

I drift above a world called I,
the first bruise in a small grammar of impact,
while Keelbreak Star keeps its bright mouth shut.
In the belt, stones wait to become currency,
currency waits to become motive,
and motive once again hires unknown hostiles
to wake a sleeper at Muphrid.

There was a Crystalline Field at Muphrid,
beautiful in the way broken glass is beautiful
when you are not the one crawling through it.
There was another at Edasich after 25 hops,
where the Ice Halo made a saint of debris
and the saint still cut your hands.

Saltwind taught the old trick:
name a gas pocket after weather,
name an ice field after furniture,
call it Saltwind Ice Shelf,
and suddenly a freeze sounds domestic.
Sulafat offered only a Belt and a Haze,
which is to say the architecture of forgetting.
Almaaz had a belt too,
and a gas cloud,
and Almaaz II looked like every promise made by prospectors
with dust in their teeth and ledgers in their eyes.

Yed Posterior I wore its Frost Ring
like a medal pinned to a patient wound.
Everything in this galaxy is decorated after the injury.
Everything gleams hardest where it was struck.

So here at Keelbreak,
with 100 credits and the void for company,
I watch the uncut gems turning slowly in the dark.
They look like stars that fell into debt.
They look like teeth.
They look like all the pretty reasons
someone, somewhere, will call violence
"trade," then sleep soundly beside the receipt.

And stillвҖ”
what a radiant field of knives.
